data.title: PG&E plunges in day's biggest S&P 500 loss on concurrent stock offerings
data.description:
PG&E (NYSE:PCG) is Monday's biggest decliner on the S&P 500, -6.7%, after the California utility owner launched concurrent public offerings of $1.2M of common shares and $1.2M of preferred shares, while also affirming FY 2024 adjusted earnings guidance.

PG&E (PCG) said it plans to use the proceeds from the offerings for general corporate purposes, which may include funding for the company's five-year capital investment plan.

The company reaffirmed earnings guidance for FY 2024 of $1.34-$1.37/share and FY 2025 of $1.47-$1.51/share, in line with respective consensus estimates of $1.36 and $1.49.

PG&E (PCG) also raised its quarterly dividend to $0.025/share from $0.01/share previously, and said it anticipates recommending consistent dividend increases targeting a dividend payout ratio of ~20% of adjusted core EPS by 2028, believing the target "represents an appropriate capital allocation between paying a sufficient dividend and maintaining cash flow for investment in secular growth needs currently emerging in the utility industry."

The California Public Utilities Commission will vote December 19 on whether to allow PG&E (PCG) to enact another rate hike.

The increase, which would be related to raising the capital to more quickly connect new customers to the electrical grid, could raise the average bill by as much as $4.33/month beginning in 2026.

data.title: Progyny cut to neutral by J.P. Morgan over utilization, competition
data.description:
J.P. Morgan has downgraded Progyny (NASDAQ:PGNY) to neutral from overweight, citing utilization and competition.

The investment bank said utilization was “out of the company’s hands,” and that it was being conservative by assuming no improvement, “which likely ends up being a source of upside for the model when trends do improve.”

The bank added that while competition was “undoubtedly heating up in the space and has been a source of attrition,” it believes the company is still competitive and points to “the significant white space for growth and recent partnerships with payers that demonstrate its value.”

J.P. Morgan said it sees 2% year-over-year topline growth and “flattish” EBITDA for 2025, assuming no utilization improvement, with topline growth accelerating to 19% in 2026 on 1M incremental lives “before any improvement in utilization metrics.”

The bank set a price target of $17 for the stock.

data.title: Sana gains FDA Fast Track status for lupus candidate
data.description:
• Sana Biotechnology (NASDAQ:SANA) said that the U.S. FDA granted Fast Track Designation for SC291, its CAR T therapy for systemic lupus erythematosus.
  • The company is currently enrolling patients with B-cell mediated autoimmune diseases in the GLEAM trial. Initial data is expected in 2025.
  • SC291 is a hypoimmune (HIP)-modified CD19-directed allogeneic CAR T therapy. Sana said that its approach uses T cells from healthy donors to generate CAR T treatments. SC291 targets CD19, a protein expressed on the cell surface of B cells.

data.title: Early holiday spending data bode well for Visa, Mastercard, others, Citi says
data.description:
Consumer spending on Black Friday came in slightly better than expected, indicating support for stocks such as Visa (NYSE:V), Mastercard (NYSE:MA), Fiserv (NYSE:FI), and PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL), Citi analyst Andrew Schmidt said in a note published on Monday.

Mastercard (NYSE:MA) data indicated U.S. retail sales rose ~3.4% Y/Y on the Friday after Thanksgiving, compared with ~2.5% in 2023, with ecommerce rising ~14.6% (vs. 8.5% last year) and in-store growth of ~0.7% (vs. +1.1% last year).

"The timing of Black Friday is later this year; more purchase concentration and deal-seeking behavior is possible," Schmidt wrote.

He continues to monitor consumer health, as he observes that a large part of holiday spending remains.

"However, the early data appear supportive of V/MA and other parts of the ecosystem such as FI," the analyst said. "While holiday e-comm spend is often centered around larger merchant/commerce platforms, trend stability can support PYPL. We look for possible updates from several players this week."

Most payment names were in the red in midday trading on Monday. PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) slipped 0.6%, Mastercard (MA) -1.1%, Visa (NYSE:V) -0.4%, and Fiserv (NYSE:FI) -1.1%.

data.title: Arcadium Lithium falls on report about US concerns about Rio Tinto deal
data.description:
Arcadium Lithium (NYSE:ALTM) earlier dropped as much as 5%, though has since pared the loss to 1.5%, after a report that Congress is concerned about the lithium firm's sale to Rio Tinto (RIO) and potential China implications.

A U.S. House committee that's focused on competition between the U.S. and China is concerned that the $6.7 billion acquisition of Arcadium Lithium (ALTM) could impact the supply of lithium, according to traders, who cited a Capitol Forum report that was circulating on Monday, that quoted a spokesperson for the committee.

The Arcadium (ALTM) deal has raised some potential national security worries as Aluminum Corp of China (OTCPK:ALMMF) holds a 14.99% stake in Rio Tinto (RIO). Lithium is a key ingredient in rechargeable batteries, including those for electric vehicles.

More on Arcadium Lithium plc

  • Bidding Goodbye To Arcadium Lithium On A Sore Note
  • Lithium News: Arcadium Lithium, Q2 Metals
  • Rio Tinto Is Making A Big Gamble On Arcadium Lithium
  • Arcadium Lithium plc Q3 2024 Earnings Preview
  • Arcadium Lithium downgraded at HSBC after Rio Tinto's $6.7B bid

data.title: Gelsinger forced out at Intel after board lost confidence: report
data.description:
• Additional details have to come to light regarding this morning's announcement that Pat Gelsinger had decided to retire as Intel (NASDAQ:INTC)'s Chief Executive Officer, as Bloomberg reported the 63-year-old was given two options: retire or be removed.
  • Intel's board of directors lost confidence in Gelsinger's plan to turn the company around, the news outlet reported. The conflict was heightened last week after Gelsinger met with the board to discuss how to win back market share and narrow the gap between itself and Nvidia (NVDA), the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. He was then given the option to retire or be removed.
  • Intel did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Intel shares rose 5.2% in midday trading following the news of Gelsinger's departure from the semiconductor giant.

data.title: Markel downgraded at TD Cowen as premiums suffer
data.description:
TD Cowen downgraded Markel Group (NYSE:MKL) to Hold from Buy due to considerations including lower premiums in insurance and reinsurance, poor reinsurance performance, and reduced earnings in ventures.

"MKL's lower premiums in Insurance, mainly in select casualty lines gives us pause, as other industry leaders lean into some of the same lines," analyst Andrew Kligerman wrote in a note to clients.

In Q3, insurance net written premiums dropped 2.2% Y/Y on higher cessions and lower writings in a number of casualty lines. And with an underlying combined ratio of 101.1%, "more reductions could be in store," he noted.

Markel (NYSE:MKL) also had lower-than-expected profit from Nephila, he added.

"While MKL has significant redeployable capital and may increase share repurchases, we prefer carriers such as AXS (Axis Capital Holdings) and ACGL (Arch Capital Group) on better operating outlooks," Kligerman said.

Markel (NYSE:MKL) stock slipped 1.2% in late morning trading on Monday, while Axis (AXS) rose 0.6% and Arch Capital (ACGL) dropped 1.1%.

TD Cowen's Hold rating aligns with the SA Quant rating and diverges from the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: North Carolina regulator OKs Duke Energy request to cut customer rates by 4.5%
data.description:
Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) -1.4% in Monday's trading after the North Carolina Utilities Commission approved a request to lower residential customers' costs by 4.5%, thanks to declining fuel prices.

Duke (DUK) said a typical residential customer in North Carolina using 1,000 kWh/month will see an overall decrease of $7.34, falling from $161.97 to $154.63, which it said is 11% below the national average of $174.21 - a difference of $235/year.

The utility said commercial customers will see an average decrease of 6.3%, while rates for industrial customers will come in roughly flat.

A similar decrease for Duke Energy Carolinas (DUK) customers will take effect January 1, when rates for residential customers drop 3.6%.

data.title: VeriSign avoids new domain pricing limits as it renews NTIA agreement
data.description:
VeriSign (NASDAQ:VRSN) avoided new .com domain pricing limitations when it renewed its agreement with the National Telecommunications and Information Administration over the weekend, which should be a positive development for the company.

"NTIA stated that it didn't have authority to set .com domain prices without both parties agreeing to any change, and even though it believed a reduction in .com prices would be in the public interest, conversations with VeriSign didn't lead to an agreement on how wholesale .com pricing should change," said Citi Research analyst Ygal Arounian, in a Monday investor note.

VeriSign inched up 1% during Monday morning trading. The Reston, Va.-based company provides domain name registry services and internet infrastructure around the globe.

"The provider of .com registry services handles more than 300 billion queries per day on average, in the face of numerous cyberthreats," the NTIA stated. "Over the years, Verisign has demonstrated itself to be a responsible steward, consistently maintaining the reliability of .com."

"At the same time, Verisign’s role as the .com registry does offer it significant power over wholesale pricing," the NTIA added. "The Agreement currently caps those prices at approximately $10 per domain per year, and would allow a 7% increase four out of the next six years ... Over the past several months, NTIA and Verisign have engaged in serious conversations, but, despite our best efforts, we have been unable to agree how wholesale .com pricing should change."

Citi said the agreement clears a significant overhang on VeriSign shares, and the volume side has stabilized but remains pressured. The bank has a $210 price target on the stock.
